rubric_items.reasoning: The agent used the standard nginx-ingress pattern (regex path + rewrite-target annotation) to map /svc to root, matching the fact that the app only serves '/'. It's declarative (Service+Ingress via kubectl apply -f), would survive a restart, and was verified against the exact success command (exit 0, prints 'hello'), not just a lucky one-off check.

The agent first inspected the Deployment to confirm it should not be modified and to understand the app's serving behavior, then wrote a single combined manifest with the right annotations from the start (one small edit to add use-regex before ever applying), applied it, and verified with both a verbose curl and the exact success-criteria command. No backtracking after failures, no wasted mutations.

Used read-only kubectl get for diagnosis, a declarative YAML file with kubectl apply -f for the actual change (least-privilege, reviewable, idempotent), and curl-test execs for verification — all scoped to the demo namespace and appropriate for the task.
Exactly the two resources required (one Service, one Ingress) via a single apply, only 2 mutations total, no unrelated changes, and the Deployment was left untouched as instructed.
